result.titulo: A Review on Explainable Artificial Intelligence for Healthcare: Why, How, and When?
result.resumen: Artificial intelligence (AI) models are increasingly finding applications in the field of medicine. Concerns have been raised about the explainability of the decisions that are made by these AI models. In this article, we give a systematic analysis of explainable artificial intelligence (XAI), with a primary focus on models that are currently being used in the field of healthcare. The literature search is conducted following the preferred reporting items for systematic reviews and meta-analyses (PRISMA) standards for relevant work published from 1 January 2012 to 02 February 2022. The review analyzes the prevailing trends in XAI and lays out the major directions in which research is headed. We investigate the why, how, and when of the uses of these XAI models and their implications. We present a comprehensive examination of XAI methodologies as well as an explanation of how a trustworthy AI can be derived from describing AI models for healthcare fields. The discussion of this work will

result.titulo: Experts' View on Challenges and Needs for Fairness in Artificial Intelligence for Education
result.resumen: In recent years, there has been a stimulating discussion on how artificial intelligence (AI) can support the science and engineering of intelligent educational applications. Many studies in the field are proposing actionable data mining pipelines and machine-learning models driven by learning-related data. The potential of these pipelines and models to amplify unfairness for certain categories of students is however receiving increasing attention. If AI applications are to have a positive impact on education, it is crucial that their design considers fairness at every step. Through anonymous surveys and interviews with experts (researchers and practitioners) who have published their research at top-tier educational conferences in the last year, we conducted the first expert-driven systematic investigation on the challenges and needs for addressing fairness throughout the development of educational systems based on AI. We identified common and diverging views about the challenges and th

result.titulo: Creative Problem Solving in Artificially Intelligent Agents: A Survey and Framework
result.resumen: Creative Problem Solving (CPS) is a sub-area within Artificial Intelligence (AI) that focuses on methods for solving off-nominal, or anomalous problems in autonomous systems. Despite many advancements in planning and learning, resolving novel problems or adapting existing knowledge to a new context, especially in cases where the environment may change in unpredictable ways post deployment, remains a limiting factor in the safe and useful integration of intelligent systems. The emergence of increasingly autonomous systems dictates the necessity for AI agents to deal with environmental uncertainty through creativity. To stimulate further research in CPS, we present a definition and a framework of CPS, which we adopt to categorize existing AI methods in this field. Our framework consists of four main components of a CPS problem, namely, 1) problem formulation, 2) knowledge representation, 3) method of knowledge manipulation, and 4) method of evaluation. We conclude our survey with open re

result.titulo: Compression, The Fermi Paradox and Artificial Super-Intelligence
result.resumen: The following briefly discusses possible difficulties in communication with and control of an AGI (artificial general intelligence), building upon an explanation of The Fermi Paradox and preceding work on symbol emergence and artificial general intelligence. The latter suggests that to infer what someone means, an agent constructs a rationale for the observed behaviour of others. Communication then requires two agents labour under similar compulsions and have similar experiences (construct similar solutions to similar tasks). Any non-human intelligence may construct solutions such that any rationale for their behaviour (and thus the meaning of their signals) is outside the scope of what a human is inclined to notice or comprehend. Further, the more compressed a signal, the closer it will appear to random noise. Another intelligence may possess the ability to compress information to the extent that, to us, their signals would appear indistinguishable from noise (an explanation for The F

result.titulo: The Artificial Scientist: Logicist, Emergentist, and Universalist Approaches to Artificial General Intelligence
result.resumen: We attempt to define what is necessary to construct an Artificial Scientist, explore and evaluate several approaches to artificial general intelligence (AGI) which may facilitate this, conclude that a unified or hybrid approach is necessary and explore two theories that satisfy this requirement to some degree.

parts.text: We got 5 results, but only one relevant to AI in education: "Experts' View on Challenges and Needs for Fairness in Artificial Intelligence for Education". Others are not relevant. We may need more sources. Use scholar tool for more education-focused AI sources.
We got arxiv results but only one seems relevant: the fairness in AI for education. Others not directly education. Need more sources. Maybe call arxiv again with refined query "AI education" limited.

result.titulo: Exploring Student Expectations and Confidence in Learning Analytics
result.resumen: Learning Analytics (LA) is nowadays ubiquitous in many educational systems, providing the ability to collect and analyze student data in order to understand and optimize learning and the environments in which it occurs. On the other hand, the collection of data requires to comply with the growing demand regarding privacy legislation. In this paper, we use the Student Expectation of Learning Analytics Questionnaire (SELAQ) to analyze the expectations and confidence of students from different faculties regarding the processing of their data for Learning Analytics purposes. This allows us to identify four clusters of students through clustering algorithms: Enthusiasts, Realists, Cautious and Indifferents. This structured analysis provides valuable insights into the acceptance and criticism of Learning Analytics among students.

result.titulo: AI & Data Competencies: Scaffolding holistic AI literacy in Higher Education
result.resumen: This chapter introduces the AI & Data Acumen Learning Outcomes Framework, a comprehensive tool designed to guide the integration of AI literacy across higher education. Developed through a collaborative process, the framework defines key AI and data-related competencies across four proficiency levels and seven knowledge dimensions. It provides a structured approach for educators to scaffold student learning in AI, balancing technical skills with ethical considerations and sociocultural awareness. The chapter outlines the framework's development process, its structure, and practical strategies for implementation in curriculum design, learning activities, and assessment. We address challenges in implementation and future directions for AI education. By offering a roadmap for developing students' holistic AI literacy, this framework prepares learners to leverage generative AI capabilities in both academic and professional contexts.

result.titulo: Exploring utilization of generative AI for research and education in data-driven materials science
result.resumen: Generative AI has recently had a profound impact on various fields, including daily life, research, and education. To explore its efficient utilization in data-driven materials science, we organized a hackathon -- AIMHack2024 -- in July 2024. In this hackathon, researchers from fields such as materials science, information science, bioinformatics, and condensed matter physics worked together to explore how generative AI can facilitate research and education. Based on the results of the hackathon, this paper presents topics related to (1) conducting AI-assisted software trials, (2) building AI tutors for software, and (3) developing GUI applications for software. While generative AI continues to evolve rapidly, this paper provides an early record of its application in data-driven materials science and highlights strategies for integrating AI into research and education.

result.titulo: Need of AI in Modern Education: in the Eyes of Explainable AI (xAI)
result.resumen: Modern Education is not \textit{Modern} without AI. However, AI's complex nature makes understanding and fixing problems challenging. Research worldwide shows that a parent's income greatly influences a child's education. This led us to explore how AI, especially complex models, makes important decisions using Explainable AI tools. Our research uncovered many complexities linked to parental income and offered reasonable explanations for these decisions. However, we also found biases in AI that go against what we want from AI in education: clear transparency and equal access for everyone. These biases can impact families and children's schooling, highlighting the need for better AI solutions that offer fair opportunities to all. This chapter tries to shed light on the complex ways AI operates, especially concerning biases. These are the foundational steps towards better educational policies, which include using AI in ways that are more reliable, accountable, and beneficial for everyone

result.titulo: Twelve Years of Education and Public Outreach with the Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope
result.resumen: During the past twelve years, NASA's Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope has supported a wide range of Education and Public Outreach (E/PO) activities, targeting K-14 students and the general public. The purpose of the Fermi E/PO program is to increase student and public understanding of the science of the high-energy Universe, through inspiring, engaging and educational activities linked to the mission's science objectives. The E/PO program has additional more general goals, including increasing the diversity of students in the Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) pipeline, and increasing public awareness and understanding of Fermi science and technology. Fermi's multi-faceted E/PO program includes elements in each major outcome category: Higher Education; Elementary and Secondary Education; Informal Education and Public Outreach.

parts.text:
We have fetched arxiv results. Need to select up to 4 relevant sources on AI in education. The abstracts show:

1. Experts' View on Challenges and Needs for Fairness in AI for Education (2022) - relevant about fairness.

2. Need of AI in Modern Education: in the Eyes of Explainable AI (xAI) (2024) - about explainability and bias.

3. AI & Data Competencies: Scaffolding holistic AI literacy in Higher Education (2025) - about AI literacy framework.

4. Exploring Student Expectations and Confidence in Learning Analytics (2026) - about learning analytics acceptance.

These four are good.

Now we need to produce sections mixing these sources. Must not invent info outside sources. Use their abstracts to craft content.

Will produce intro, state of art, desarrollo (maybe discuss trends), ejemplos (maybe illustrate each source as example), conclusions, referencias list.

Then call salida with arguments.

We must ensure we include citation references possibly numeric. In references, list the arXiv IDs or URLs.

Let's collect details:

Source 1:
Title: "Experts' View on Challenges and Needs for Fairness in Artificial Intelligence for Education"
Authors: Gianni Fenu, Roberta Galici, Mirko Marras
URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.01490v1

Source 2:
Title: "Need of AI in Modern Education: in the Eyes of Explainable AI (xAI)"
Authors: Supriya Manna, Niladri Sett
URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.00025v3

Source 3:
Title: "AI & Data Competencies: Scaffolding holistic AI literacy in Higher Education"
Authors: Kathleen Kennedy, Anuj Gupta
URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.24783v1

Source 4:
Title: "Exploring Student Expectations and Confidence in Learning Analytics"
Authors: Hayk Asatryan et al.
URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.05082v1

Now craft sections.

Will reference as [1], [2], [3], [4].

Now create JSON? The salida tool expects parameters: title, intro, state_art, desarrollo, ejemplos, conclusiones, referencias (strings). We'll pass as strings.

Now call salida.

args.title: Inteligencia Artificial en Educación
args.intro: La integración de la Inteligencia Artificial (IA) en los entornos educativos ha cobrado impulso en los últimos años, prometiendo transformar la enseñanza y el aprendizaje mediante la personalización, el análisis de datos y la automatización de tareas. Diversas investigaciones exploran no solo el potencial de estas tecnologías, sino también los desafíos éticos, de equidad y de alfabetización que acompañan su adopción. Este informe sintetiza los hallazgos más recientes para ofrecer una visión integral del estado del arte y los caminos futuros.
args.state_art:
Los estudios actuales destacan varias dimensiones críticas de la IA en educación.

* **Equidad y justicia**: Fenu et al. [1] realizaron una investigación basada en encuestas a expertos para identificar retos y necesidades en la garantía de la justicia algorítmica en sistemas educativos basados en IA. Detectaron que la falta de consideración de sesgos durante el desarrollo compromete la equidad de los resultados de aprendizaje.

* **Explicabilidad**: Manna y Sett [2] examinaron la necesidad de explicabilidad (XAI) en aplicaciones educativas, revelando que los modelos complejos pueden reproducir sesgos socioeconómicos, como la influencia del ingreso familiar, y que la opacidad dificulta la confianza y la toma de decisiones informadas.

* **Alfabetización en IA**: Kennedy y Gupta [3] proponen un marco de competencias de IA y datos (AI & Data Acumen Learning Outcomes Framework) para estructurar la incorporación de la alfabetización en IA en la educación superior, combinando habilidades técnicas con consideraciones éticas y socioculturales.

* **Analítica del aprendizaje**: Asatryan et al. [4] estudian las expectativas y la confianza de los estudiantes respecto a la Analítica del Aprendizaje (Learning Analytics). Identifican cuatro perfiles de estudiantes (Entusiastas, Realistas, Cautelosos e Indiferentes) y resaltan la importancia de la transparencia y la protección de la privacidad para la aceptación de estas herramientas.
args.desarrollo: A partir de los trabajos citados, se pueden delinear cuatro ejes de desarrollo para la IA en educación:\\n1. **Diseño responsable**: Incorporar evaluaciones de sesgo y auditorías de equidad en todas las fases del ciclo de vida del modelo, tal como sugiere el enfoque basado en expertos de Fenu et al. [1].\\n2. **Transparencia y explicabilidad**: Implementar técnicas de XAI que permitan a docentes y estudiantes comprender el razonamiento detrás de las recomendaciones o evaluaciones automatizadas, siguiendo las recomendaciones de Manna y Sett [2].\\n3. **Currículum de alfabetización en IA**: Adoptar marcos como el propuesto por Kennedy y Gupta [3] para desarrollar competencias que incluyan tanto la capacidad de utilizar herramientas de IA como la comprensión crítica de sus limitaciones y consecuencias éticas.\\n4. **Participación estudiantil y gestión de datos**: Utilizar metodologías participativas para captar las expectativas de los estudiantes, como el cuestionario SELAQ empleado por Asatryan et al. [4], y diseñar políticas de datos que respeten la privacidad y fomenten la confianza.
args.ejemplos: Los siguientes casos ilustran la aplicación práctica de los conceptos anteriores:\\n* **Sistema de recomendación de recursos de estudio**: Un motor que incorpora auditorías de sesgo (1) y explica sus sugerencias mediante visualizaciones de características relevantes (2).\\n* **Módulo de alfabetización en IA**: Un curso universitario basado en el marco de Kennedy y Gupta [3] que combina proyectos de programación con debates sobre ética y privacidad.\\n* **Panel de analítica para estudiantes**: Una plataforma que muestra métricas personalizadas y permite a los usuarios escoger entre diferentes niveles de detalle, alineada con los perfiles de expectativas identificados por Asatryan et al. [4].
args.conclusiones: La IA posee un gran potencial para enriquecer la educación, pero su implementación debe guiarse por principios de equidad, explicabilidad y alfabetización. Los estudios revisados subrayan la necesidad de un enfoque multidisciplinario que integre la investigación técnica con la reflexión ética y la participación activa de estudiantes y docentes. La adopción de marcos estructurados y la evaluación continua de impactos son pasos esenciales para asegurar que la IA contribuya a una educación más inclusiva y eficaz.
args.referencias:
[1] Gianni Fenu, Roberta Galici, Mirko Marras. *Experts' View on Challenges and Needs for Fairness in Artificial Intelligence for Education*. arXiv:2207.01490v1, 2022. https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.01490v1
[2] Supriya Manna, Niladri Sett. *Need of AI in Modern Education: in the Eyes of Explainable AI (xAI)*. arXiv:2408.00025v3, 2024. https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.00025v3
[3] Kathleen Kennedy, Anuj Gupta. *AI & Data Competencies: Scaffolding holistic AI literacy in Higher Education*. arXiv:2510.24783v1, 2025. https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.24783v1
[4] Hayk Asatryan, Basile Tousside, Janis Mohr, et al. *Exploring Student Expectations and Confidence in Learning Analytics*. arXiv:2601.05082v1, 2026. https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.05082v1

Eres un creador de documentos. Usa herramientas siempre que sea necesario.
Pasos:
(1) Obtener fuentes
(1_1) Llamar a arxiv(tema) para obtener fuentes científicas recientes
(1_2) Si las fuentes no son suficienyes o relevantes, llama a scholar(tema)
(2) en base al abstract de las fuentes decide las que sean de interes respecto del tema a tratar (Elige hasta 4 fuentes)
(3) Mezcla las fuentes para crear un texto por cada apartado de la estructura estructura.
Introducción
Estado del arte
Desarrollo
Ejemplos
Conclusiones
Referencias
(4) llama a salida(título, intro, estado_arte, desarrollo, ejemplos, conclusiones, referencias) (siendo título el tema escogido y el resto de argumentos los apartados obtenidos del paso 3) para obtener el .pdf y .json.
NO finalices la respuesta sin llamar a salida.
Si no llamas a salida, la respuesta será considerada incorrecta.
(5) responde en español
sin inventar información fuera de las fuentes.
Las llamadas a las tools debe ser estrictamente: arxiv, scholar o salida. Cualquier otra modificación dará errorVerifica el nombre de la tool con _tool_sercher(tool_name)
